text: Code reviews are an essential part of software development. In simple terms, a code review is when a developer checks another colleague's code before it gets added to the main project. This process is very valuable for several reasons. First, it helps catch bugs early, before they can cause bigger problems in the final product. Additionally, reviewing code is a great way for knowledge sharing. Team members can learn new techniques and understand different parts of a project. Ultimately, regular code reviews improve the overall team quality, creating more reliable software and a stronger, more collaborative team.
